Scorching Heatwave Engulfs Rajasthan: Temperatures Soar Above Normal
Rajasthan experienced a relentless heatwave as temperatures surged across the state, with Churu recording the highest at 42.8°C. Other regions like Chittorgarh and Barmer also faced scorching heat, causing disruptions in daily life as residents took protective measures against the intense sun.
Rajasthan is gripped by a severe heatwave, with mercury levels rising well above normal in several areas on Saturday.
Churu led the temperature chart at 42.8 degrees Celsius, surpassing the norm by 3.1 degrees. Cities such as Chittorgarh, Barmer, and Kota followed closely, resulting in a heated weekend across the state.
This intense sunlight affected daily routines, as residents adopted various measures like using umbrellas to combat the oppressive heat, highlighting the broader impact of the soaring temperatures on communities.
(With inputs from agencies.)
